rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, ensuring visible identifiers are properly scoped.
Use only visible AADL features or locally declared helpers in AGREE expressions to avoid type resolution errors.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid and type-consistent, using only declared or visible identifiers.
Component type annexes are preferred for assumptions and guarantees involving component ports.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers unless explicitly imported or available in the model.
Ensure that all expressions in AGREE contracts match the expected scalar types (int, real, bool).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions use `assume "label": expression;`, guarantees use `guarantee "label": expression;`.
Equations and constants are declared with `eq name : type = expression;` and `const name : type = value;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not visible or imported in the current AADL scope.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes instead.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package visibility
AGREE validator diagnostics (empty in this case)
Implementation-level bindings in component type contracts
